patch_type: Evidence-to-score impact preview over V60.1 Evidence State Transition Prototype
release_chain_status: V60.2 is the current implemented build. It loads after V60.1 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It shows how evidence state transitions would affect trust score, confidence, reliance risk, runtime admissibility, and Proof Pack defensibility without claiming live scoring. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.2. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
uploaded_requirements_source: CyberShield Trust Model Registry and Scoring Requirements uploaded by Dr. Justice; controlling product doctrine for scoring/model work.
v60_2_changes: shows static score impact previews for evidence state changes
separates trust score delta from confidence delta
shows reliance risk may stay high even if trust improves
shows Proof Pack defensibility change preview
preserves V60.1 Evidence State Transition Prototype
preserves V59 Internet Trust Engine scaffold and guardrails

evidence_to_score_impact_preview.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. Score impact previews are illustrative and not live calculations. No backend persistence, live scoring, live evidence retrieval, automated verification, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, or enforcement is performed.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3 Universal Model Trace Inspector
goals: expose one common inspection pattern for any score, evidence item, decision record, TrustMap object, runtime action, Proof Pack recommendation, or Internet Trust claim row
show model name, version, score, confidence, evidence, missing evidence, assumptions, reliance risk, runtime action, owner, and next step
preserve prototype boundaries and avoid live scoring claims

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
implemented_release_train.v59.5: Internet Trust QA and Copy Guardrail Pass

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Score impact previews are illustrative and not live calculations
V60.2 does not persist evidence to a backend
V60.2 does not retrieve live evidence
V60.2 does not perform live scoring
V60.2 does not create tickets or send notifications
V60.2 does not perform workflow automation
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
